issue_title: Exception due to requirement of "client_id" presence within Request Object
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Issue Description:
===================

A thirdparty OIDC client registered in Keycloak is using the authorisation code grant flow.
The authorization request is build by this OIDC client and the `request_uri` request parameter is provided.

When Keycloak tries to handle the JWE Encrypted Request Object obtained through the "request_uri" request parameter, we can see that Keycloak verifies that the "client_id" claim is present within the decrypted request object and is the same as the "client_id" provided request parameter. Request Object provided by a thirdparty OIDC Client doesn't contain this claim as this is not a mandatory claim in the OIDC specifications.

The OIDC specification states that for the request to be a valid OAuth 2.0 Authorization Request, " values for the response_type and client_id parameters MUST be included using the OAuth 2.0 request syntax, since they are REQUIRED by OAuth 2.0. The values for these parameters MUST match those in the Request Object, if present."
[OIDC Specification](
https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#RequestUriParameter
)

The "response_type" and "client_id" request parameters are indeed provided by this thirdparty OIDC client but are not present in the Request Object because these values are optional within it. So the validation against the "client_id" should be done only if the claim is provided in the Request Object only... and then the presence of this claim shouldn't be mandatory as today within Keycloak

**Auth Request Endpoint**
https://www.foo.com/authorize?response_type=code
&redirect_uri=https%3A%2F%2Frfoo.com&request_uri=https%3A%2F%2Ffoo.com/bar&scope=openid%2Bservices&client_id=aaa&state=bbb&nonce=ccc

**Decrypted Request Object**
```
{
"foo": "bar",
"baz": "some"
}
```

server.log
===========

```
org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.request.AuthorizationEndpointRequestParserProcessor.parseRequest(AuthorizationEndpointRequestParserProcessor.java:96)
at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.AuthorizationEndpoint.process(AuthorizationEndpoint.java:132)
at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.AuthorizationEndpoint.buildGet(AuthorizationEndpoint.java:112)
at sun.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method)
at sun.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(NativeMethodAccessorImpl.java:62)
at sun.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.java:43)
at java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:498)
```

Notes:
=====

The change should be done in the following class
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/endpoints/request/AuthzEndpointRequestObjectParser.java
In particular...

```
if (clientId == null) {
throw new RuntimeException("Request object must be set with the client_id");
}

if (!client.getClientId().equals(clientId.asText())) {
throw new RuntimeException("The client_id in the request object is not the same as the authorizing client");
}
```

should be changed into

```
if (clientId != null && !client.getClientId().equals(clientId.asText())) {
throw new RuntimeException("The client_id in the request object is not the same as the authorizing client");
}
```

Workaround
===========
Updated source file of AuthzEndpointRequestObjectParser
Updated source file of AuthzEndpointRequestParser
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/endpoints/request/AuthzEndpointRequestParser.java
 
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/endpoints/request/AuthzEndpointRequestObjectParser.java

Queries:
=========
1) Is the "client_id" claim mandatory in the object request (current Keycloak mode) ?

2) Is the overriding of each authorization endpoint request attribute, with the one available from the one in the object request even if it is null, correct (current Keycloak mode) ?

3) Regarding the RFC9101 ("The OAuth 2.0 Authorization Framework: JWT-Secured Authorization Request (JAR)"), the "client_id" claim seems indeed mandatory.
However, it doesn't seem correct to override the authorization endpoint request attributes with null values if not present in the request object.

### Version

19.0.1

### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Actual behavior

_No response_

### How to Reproduce?

_No response_

### Anything else?

_No response_

I do x509 client certificate authentication with french healthcare pki.

I use the source "Subject's common name" in the configuration to identify the user.

But, i have some issue with the certificat of this pki.

The Relative Distinguished Name (RDN) which contain CN is multi-valued (SN=surname+CN=common name+GN=given name) and the actual code just take first element.
So Surname (SN) is extracted instead of Common name (CN).

issue_title: Delay LDAPObject creation until mandatory attributes are set
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

If the `syncRegistrations` option is enabled in an LDAP user federation (in order to create users in the LDAP server as soon as they are created in keycloak) current implementation works this way:

1. When the `UserModel` is created at keycloak it is also added in LDAP only with the username and default values provided by the mappers (`UserAttributeLDAPStorageMapper`). It is created at [this point](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/18.0.2/federation/ldap/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/ldap/LDAPStorageProvider.java#L289) calling the method `addUserToLdap`.
2. In the [addUserToLdap](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/18.0.2/federation/ldap/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/ldap/LDAPUtils.java#L72-L78) the `onRegisterUserToLDAP` method is called for each mapper and the default values are set for the LDAPObject to create.
3. The problem here is that the user object is added to the LDAP server with only the username and the default values. The current code forces [a default empty value](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/18.0.2/federation/ldap/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/ldap/mappers/UserAttributeLDAPStorageMapper.java#L460-L463) for all the mandatory attributes.
4. After the creation the `UserModel` is modified setting the different attributes using typical setters (setFirstName, setEmail, setAttribute,...) and the UserModel and in turn the LDAPObject are modified accordingly.
5. When the transaction is committed the LDAP user is modified accordingly setting the final values.

Let's give an example, having an LDAP configuration with `syncRegistrations` with the following mandatory attributes and no default value (empty value will be used for all of them):

* username - uid
* firstname - cn
* lastname - sn
* email - mail

If the user is created with this `kcadm` command:

```
./kcadm.sh create users -r ldap -s username=ricky -s firstName=ricky -s lastName=ricky -s email=ricky@lala.org
```

First the user is created in LDAP with the following data:

```
DN: uid=ricky,dc=example,dc=com
mail:  
uid: ricky
objectclass: inetOrgPerson
sn:
cn:
```

And at the end of the transaction the entry is modified to the final values provided:

```
DN: uid=ricky,dc=example,dc=com
mail: ricky@lala.org
uid: ricky
sn: ricky
cn: ricky
```

The behavior is weird but the way `UserModel` works forced doing it this way. It's impossible to know when the transaction will finish (and the LDAP object needs to be created before the transaction ends, in order to get the DN and the GUID attribute of the entry created to set them into the `UserModel`). Problems:

1. The RDN attribute can be defined to another attribute different to the username (mail from example) and the entry will be created first with the RDN `mail=defaultvalue` and then renamed to the correct passed mail. This can fail if two users are created at the same time.
2. Any LDAP attribute can be configured as unique and it can fail when using the default attribute (see for example in [i389](https://directory.fedoraproject.org/docs/389ds/howto/howto-uid-uniqueness.html)). It's the same as the RDN, it can fail if two entries are created at the same time (default value will not be unique and one creation would fail).

The solution is delaying the creation of the LDAPObject for the user until all the mandatory attributes are set. As soon as the LDAPObject is complete (in mandatory attrs) the entry will be created. At the end of the transaction the entry will be modified for the rest of attributes (if any other non mandatory attribute is added after all mandatory attributes are set). The modification should ensure backwards compatibility with the current behavior.






### Version

19.0.1

### Expected behavior

See description.

### Actual behavior

See description.

### How to Reproduce?

Configure an LDAP provider with values:

```
syncRegistrations: true
importEnabled: true
usernameLDAPAttribute: uid
usersDn: dc=example,dc=com
userObjectClasses: inetOrgPerson, organizationalPerson
rdnLDAPAttribute: mail
editMode: WRITABLE
```

And add and LDAP mapper for email:

```
providerId: user-attribute-ldap-mapper
name: email
ldap.attribute: mail
user.model.attribute: email
is.mandatory.in.ldap: true
```

When you create the user you will see the steps commented if setting ldap debug (`--log-level=INFO,org.keycloak.storage.ldap:ALL`)



### Anything else?

_No response_

Closes #14286

issue_title: KEYCLOAK_SESSION not working for some user federation setups
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

In some cases with user federation, the ID of the user can contain some special characters. For example when configured LDAP provider with "Import Users" set to OFF and with "UUID LDAP Attribute" set to "uid" . Then when you have some user with special character in the "uid", this is in turn used as the ID of the user. And this is saved in the KEYCLOAK_SESSION cookie:
```
KEYCLOAK_SESSION=**:spécial.char/**; 
KEYCLOAK_SESSION_LEGACY=***; 
```
The character "é" is present in the cookie name, which is not allowed by the undertow server. In the log is this (likely when accessing Keycloak from some Javascript application when the particular user with special character is logged):
```
ERROR [io.undertow.request] (default task-2) UT005023: Exception handling request to /auth/realms/defence-intra/protocol
/openid-connect/login-status-iframe.html: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: UT000173: An invalid control character [233] was 
present in the cookie value or attribute
```

### Version

19.0.1

### Anything else?

For the fix, it is probably easiest to Base64Url encode the cookie `KEYCLOAK_SESSION` (and maybe some other related cookies, not yet sure for which of them is this applicable) to make sure that there are not special characters in these cookies.

issue_title: Keycloak server partial gziped resources in some cases
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

We are serving keycloak theme resources behind CDN.  We have observed partial files been cached occationally.
Looks like the current implementation of https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/encoding/GzipResourceEncodingProvider.java#L42 makes it possible to have a race condition. Request close to the one initially creating the gziped resource can serve partial content. In the general case the subsequent requests will use the complete file. In case of CDN it might take a while for the cached resource to expire

### Version

18.0.0

### Expected behavior

Keycloak should serve correct and complete resource files

### Actual behavior

When concurrent requests for gziped resource are being handled, it is possible to serve partial resource 

### How to Reproduce?

Running multiple requests at single resource requesting gzipped content

### Anything else?

_No response_

Use temporary file to reduce the chance of serving partial gzipped resource (#14511)

Closes #14510

issue_title: [CVE-2022-38750] Stack-based Buffer Overflow vulnerability in org.yaml:snakeyaml
issue_body:
SnakeYAML is a transitive dependency coming from quarkus-kubernetes-client. The mitigation is to upgrade the `quarkus.operator.sdk.version` to 4.0.1 and `quarkus.version` to 2.12.2 inside https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/operator/pom.xml. 

-   Package Manager: maven
-   Vulnerable module: org.yaml:snakeyaml
-   Introduced through: org.keycloak:keycloak-operator@999-SNAPSHOT, io.quarkus:[quarkus-kubernetes-client@2.7.6.Final](mailto:quarkus-kubernetes-client@2.7.6.Final) and others

### Detailed paths

-   _Introduced through_: org.keycloak:keycloak-operator@999-SNAPSHOT › io.quarkus:[quarkus-kubernetes-client@2.7.6.Final](mailto:quarkus-kubernetes-client@2.7.6.Final) › io.smallrye.config:smallrye-config-source-yaml@2.9.2 › org.yaml:snakeyaml@1.30

# Overview

[org.yaml:snakeyaml](https://code.google.com/p/snakeyaml/source/browse/) is a YAML 1.1 parser and emitter for Java.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Stack-based Buffer Overflow when parsing crafted untrusted YAML files, which can lead to a denial-of-service.

# Remediation

Upgrade `org.yaml:snakeyaml` to version 1.31 or higher.

# References

-   [Bitbucket Commit](https://bitbucket.org/snakeyaml/snakeyaml/commits/f3ab4e0f54c37ddb10f00b71d04187bb0ef1799c)
-   [Bitbucket Issue](https://bitbucket.org/snakeyaml/snakeyaml/issues/530/stackoverflow-oss-fuzz-47039)
-   [Chromium Bugs](https://bugs.chromium.org/p/oss-fuzz/issues/detail?id=47039)

issue_body:
# Overview

[com.squareup.okhttp3:okhttp](https://mvnrepository.com/artifact/com.squareup.okhttp3/okhttp) is a HTTP & HTTP/2 client for Android and Java applications and a transitive dependency coming from OpenShift REST Client.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Information Exposure. When there's an illegal character in a header value, an `IllegalArgumentException` is thrown whose message includes the full header value.

Upgrade OpenShift REST Client fixed it on 9.0.5.Final release which can be a breaking change for us.

issue_title: External IDP tokens are not refreshed automatically for OAuth2 & OIDC IDPs when retrieving the external token
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

We're using Github (specifically a Github app) as an external IDP. We have configured Github to use expiring access tokens (good for 8 hours). When we want to get data from Github on behalf of the user, such as a list of repositories where the Github app is installed, we need a Github [user-to-server access token](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/apps/installations#list-app-installations-accessible-to-the-user-access-token).

We can retrieve these external IDP tokens from keycloak via [this endpoint](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/#retrieving-external-idp-tokens).

Unfortunately, if it has been more than 8 hours, then the access token is expired and the request to Github will fail. If I use the refresh token (which is also returned from the endpoint above), to get new tokens from Github, this works _once_. I get a new access and refresh token, and I can make a request to Github with the access token. However, this _expires_ the refresh token that Keycloak has. And there doesn't seem to be a way for me to give Keycloak the new access/refresh tokens that I've retrieved on behalf of the user.

Is this just an oversight? Is there any way to use Github as an external IDP with expiring access tokens?

### Version

19.0.2

### Expected behavior

1. Keycloak refreshes tokens _before_ returning them from `GET /realms/{realm}/broker/{provider_alias}/token` (provided the current `refresh_token` has not expired).

### Actual behavior

Keycloak appears to just return the exact response that it originally receives from Github, which is most likely an expired access token. If the refresh token is still good, it is worthless because using it will expire the one held in Keycloak.

### How to Reproduce?

Use Github as an external IDP with expiring tokens and try to use the endpoint as described above.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: MapUserProvider in KC20 needs to store username compatible with KC19 to be no-downtime-upgradable
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

PR #13183 introduced a new searchable field USERNAME_CASE_INSENSITIVE that stores for the JPA store the username in lower case, and preserves the case for the USERNAME field.

To be no-downtime-upgradable, the two versions KC19 and KC20 would need to do same things: 

KC19: 
* Writes USERNAME with always Java's lowercase to the database for new entities
* When searching for a user, applies case-insensitive search to USERNAME field using ILIKE
* When reading a version 2 entity, will write the entity in version 1 

KC20: 
* Has REALM_ATTR_USERNAME_CASE_SENSITIVE_DEFAULT set to "false"
* Writes USERNAME always with the original case
* When searching for a user, applies Java's lowercase to the search string and compares that to the USERNAME_CASE_INSENSITIVE for version 2 entities, and to  USERNAME for version 1 entities.
  :warning: so this requires all USERNAMEs to be lowercase on version 1 entities :warning:

Scenario that is problematic (tested with JPA store): 
* Create a new user with KC20 with a mixed-case-username (creating a V2 entity)
* Updating the user with KC19 - this will preserve the mixed case username, and write the entity as a V1 entity
* Login on KC20 fails, as it would search for a username that is all lowercase in the field USERNAME in the database.
  :musical_note: this will be "healed" once the user is changed on KC20, writing the entity as a V2 to the database :musical_note:

### Version

20.0.0-SNAPSHOT

### Expected behavior

When using the new store, two versions of KC should be allowed to run in parallel and should provider meaningful results, in this case users can be created in both versions and then log in or modify their user with both instances.

### Actual behavior

As described above, this could lead to a user not logging in in the new version of Keycloak.

### Anything else?

Once an existing field is filled with different data, this can lead to unexpected results.

Could be fixed by a KC19 patch release that always ensure that the username is lowercase (especially on downgrades), or in KC20 if that would use ILIKE on usernames as the old version did.

Returning a username in mixed case in the user list is also a change in behavior, yet I wasn't able to create a failing scenario from this.

Closes #14678

issue_title: User Session Count Limiter not working for some users
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Hi, 
We recently implemented a new Authentication flow with an "User Session Count Limiter" step to put a hard limit for each user in the realm at 50 sessions. 
This was tested with an account and seemed to work properly, each session after the 50th was terminating the oldest without any issue. 
However, now that we've deployed this flow in our production server, we noticed that some users still manage to go above this limit. We tried to logout all their sessions but they still manage to get to way more than 50 sessions ( above 1000 for some ). 
The limit was added on the Direct Grant Flow and the Browser Flow.
And it doesn't seem that they use another flow to bypass this, since we are getting the following logs : `[org.keycloak.authentication.authenticators.sessionlimits.UserSessionLimitsAuthenticator] (executor-thread-6727) Too many session in this realm for the current user. Session count: 299`

This feels like a bug to me but if we are missing anything or can provide any more information, please tell us so.
Thanks in advance !

### Version

18.0.0

### Expected behavior

No users should have a session count above 50.

### Actual behavior

Many users are getting sessions count above 50 (up to 1200+) 

### How to Reproduce?

We haven't been able to reproduce this with our own accounts.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Paging for "Users in role" is not guaranteed to work with JPA
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

The "Users in role" query does not specify an "ORDER BY" clause.

Hence, it is not guaranteed that the users are returned in the same order for subsequent requests, which may lead to duplicate or missing data when using paging.

This issue affects both the REST API and the Admin UI.

### Version

19.0.2

### Expected behavior

Results should always be returned in the same order.

### Actual behavior

Results are returned in "random" order (not guaranteed order defined by the database).

### How to Reproduce?

Could not reproduce with both mssql and h2, probably due to database caching.

What I found is: the query built by Hibernate looks like this (when just using the "max" parameter): 
```
    select
        userentity1_.ID as ID1_73_,
        userentity1_.CREATED_TIMESTAMP as CREATED_2_73_,
        userentity1_.EMAIL as EMAIL3_73_,
        userentity1_.EMAIL_CONSTRAINT as EMAIL_CO4_73_,
        userentity1_.EMAIL_VERIFIED as EMAIL_VE5_73_,
        userentity1_.ENABLED as ENABLED6_73_,
        userentity1_.FEDERATION_LINK as FEDERATI7_73_,
        userentity1_.FIRST_NAME as FIRST_NA8_73_,
        userentity1_.LAST_NAME as LAST_NAM9_73_,
        userentity1_.NOT_BEFORE as NOT_BEF10_73_,
        userentity1_.REALM_ID as REALM_I11_73_,
        userentity1_.SERVICE_ACCOUNT_CLIENT_LINK as SERVICE12_73_,
        userentity1_.USERNAME as USERNAM13_73_ 
    from
        USER_ROLE_MAPPING userrolema0_ cross 
    join
        USER_ENTITY userentity1_ 
    where
        userrolema0_.ROLE_ID=? 
        and userentity1_.ID=userrolema0_.USER_ID limit ?
```

It's missing an "ORDER BY" clause.

 

### Anything else?

### Background information
Query pagination with Hibernate is further described in Vlad Mihalcea's article https://vladmihalcea.com/query-pagination-jpa-hibernate/. There he states:

> The use of ORDER BY is mandatory when using query pagination because SQL does not guarantee any particular order unless we provide one via the ORDER BY clause.

### Implementation hints
The named query which is missing the ORDER BY clause is called "usersInRole": https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/bfce612641a70e106b20b136431f0e4046b5c37f/model/jpa/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/jpa/entities/UserRoleMappingEntity.java#L37

Furthermore, I found that the MapUserProvider orders by username ascending: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/a62e98f966d269baa14ba361e77d52b72134319b/model/map/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/map/user/MapUserProvider.java#L727

It would be great if both implementations are aligned with each other. What about just adding " order by u.username" to the named query "usersInRole"?
commit_message:
Fix paging on the "Users in role" endpoint, when JPA persistence is used

- add order-by-clause to the corresponding JPA query (ordering by username ASC)
- adjust admin-client RoleResource to return a List instead of a Set, by introducing new methods #getUserMembers (instead of #getRoleUserMembers - the "Role" prefix is not needed, because it is clear from the resource name that it's about roles)
- adjust tests to use the new method and check that the expected order is returned

Closes #14772

issue_title: Mail verification isn't working for multiple accounts in one session (only on auto login by clicking the verification mail, not by logging in with the credentials)
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

If a user is registering multiple accounts (different accounts with different usernames/mails etc.) at the same time, they try to confirm the verification for all their accounts.

When the user is confirming the first mail, he is automatically logged in with these account (for example, account (A)).
But now he's unable to confirm the second account (B).
Because keycloak is accepting the verification mail, but giving an error response "the user is already logged in".

The strange thing about that is, if a user (account (A)) is logged in with their credentials*, he can confirm the mail for the second account (B).
*(by typing in their credentials and not automatically logged in through the verification process)

All accounts are independent and have correlation.

### Version

16 - 19.0.3

### Expected behavior

Both behaviors should be equal.

If a user is confirming their account (A) he should be logged in automatically (works right now).
And he should be able to confirm other account (B) by clicking the verification mail.

### Actual behavior

So, the behaviors for confirming a mail it's different, based on the origin.
If the user is logged in by using the login page (using their credentials, account (A)), verifying another account (B) is possible.
If the user is logged in automatically (using the confirmation mail from account (A)), verifying another account (B) is NOT possible.

### How to Reproduce?

You need to register two accounts and verify them in a sequence:
- Enable account registration and mail verification for realm
- Register a new account (A)
- Register a new account (B)
- Click on the verification mail for account (A)
- Click on the verification mail for account (B)
- See that account (B) can not be verified because "the user is already logged in"

### Anything else?

The verification process itself is working.
It's only a problem that a user needs to log out to be able to confirm other accounts than itself.
And the weird thing by that is, that there are different behaviors for the same process.

With account (A) and account (B) I mean entirely different accounts, different username, different mail etc.
commit_message:
Do not allow verifying email from a different account

issue_title: [CVE-2022-42003] A Denial of Service (DoS) vulnerability in com.fasterxml.jackson.core:jackson-databind
issue_body:
## Status: Not affected

Depends on:
- https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/issues/28437

# Overview

[com.fasterxml.jackson.core:jackson-databind](https://github.com/FasterXML/jackson-databind) is a library which contains the general-purpose data-binding functionality and tree-model for Jackson Data Processor. At the moment 

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS) in the `_deserializeWrappedValue()` function in `StdDeserializer.java`, due to resource exhaustion when processing deeply nested arrays.

**NOTE:** This vulnerability is only exploitable when the non-default `UNWRAP_SINGLE_VALUE_ARRAYS` feature is enabled.

# Details

Denial of Service (DoS) describes a family of attacks, all aimed at making a system inaccessible to its intended and legitimate users.

Unlike other vulnerabilities, DoS attacks usually do not aim at breaching security. Rather, they are focused on making websites and services unavailable to genuine users resulting in downtime.

One popular Denial of Service vulnerability is DDoS (a Distributed Denial of Service), an attack that attempts to clog network pipes to the system by generating a large volume of traffic from many machines.

When it comes to open source libraries, DoS vulnerabilities allow attackers to trigger such a crash or crippling of the service by using a flaw either in the application code or from the use of open source libraries.

Two common types of DoS vulnerabilities:

-   High CPU/Memory Consumption- An attacker sending crafted requests that could cause the system to take a disproportionate amount of time to process. For example, [commons-fileupload:commons-fileupload](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/security/code-scanning/SNYK-JAVA-COMMONSFILEUPLOAD-30082).
    
-   Crash - An attacker sending crafted requests that could cause the system to crash. For Example, [npm `ws` package](https://snyk.io/vuln/npm:ws:20171108)
   
# Remediation

Upgrade `com.fasterxml.jackson.core:jackson-databind` to version 2.14.0-rc1 or higher.

# References

-   [Chromium Bugs](https://bugs.chromium.org/p/oss-fuzz/issues/detail?id=51020)
-   [Documentation](https://fasterxml.github.io/jackson-databind/javadoc/2.9/com/fasterxml/jackson/databind/DeserializationFeature.html#UNWRAP_SINGLE_VALUE_ARRAYS)
-   [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/FasterXML/jackson-databind/commit/d78d00ee7b5245b93103fef3187f70543d67ca33)
-   [GitHub Issue](https://github.com/FasterXML/jackson-databind/issues/3590)

# Detailed paths

-   _Introduced through_: org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server-app@999-SNAPSHOT › org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server@999-SNAPSHOT › io.quarkus:[quarkus-reactive-routes@2.12.2.Final](mailto:quarkus-reactive-routes@2.12.2.Final) › io.quarkus:[quarkus-jackson@2.12.2.Final](mailto:quarkus-jackson@2.12.2.Final) › com.fasterxml.jackson.core:jackson-databind@2.13.3

issue_title: Error when using similar keys with different algorithms in a jwks for identity provider signature validation
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

After upgrade to 19.0.2 from keycloak 18.0.1:

When using a jwks endpoint with similar keys that have the same 'kid', kty" and 'use', but different algorithms, the last key is always chosen for signature validation and the following error occurs:

2022-10-03 08:50:41,547 DEBUG [org.keycloak.keys.infinispan.InfinispanPublicKeyStorageProvider] (executor-thread-2) Public keys retrieved successfully for model 145bab56-6a3a-4c79-9ae6-8086c07959d1::idp::f5bb6a5e-2a06-49fc-ac0d-40aef190fb3e. New kids: [nrzqxULxLmRsNrpDAe0snB+cr6M=, b+wtPGa0Kda47jXT5uVpiY2kh+k=]
2022-10-03 08:50:41,547 DEBUG [org.keycloak.broker.oidc.OIDCIdentityProvider] (executor-thread-2) Failed to verify token: 	: Key with algorithm RS512 and type RSA is incorrect for provider algorithm RS256
at org.keycloak.crypto.SignatureProvider.checkKeyForVerification(SignatureProvider.java:33)

For example, a key provider with algorithm RS256 is configured, the key with algorithm RS512 (last entry in the jwks) will be chosen from the jwks and a verification exception will be thrown.

### Version

19.0.2

### Expected behavior

This error started occurring after an upgrade from Keycloak 18.0.1 to Keyloak 19.0.2. 
The expected behavior is that for similar keys defined in the openam jwks, the key with the correct algorithm that matches an available Keycloak key provider will be selected. 

### Actual behavior

The last key is always selected for signature verification from a list of keys in a jwks with similar 'use', 'kid' and 'kty' but different 'alg' (algorithm). The other keys are not included in the list fetched from from the jwks.
Bug appears to be in the way org.keycloak.util.JWKSUtils.getKeyWrappersForUse(JSONWebKeySet keySet, JWK.Use requestedUse) creates a map of keys.


### How to Reproduce?

- Setup an identity provider in keycloak configured to use a jwks URL and to validate signatures.
- Create a jwks with list of keys with similar 'kid', 'kty', 'use' but different 'alg' (the other key parts can also be similar across the keys) using for example openam-provider (see end of this 
comment for an example list with dummy keys). Let the last key in the list of similar keys have an algorithm for which there is NO key provider setup in Keycloak.
- When attempting any operation that requires jwt signature validation, the following error occurs:

DEBUG [org.keycloak.broker.oidc.OIDCIdentityProvider] (executor-thread-2) Failed to verify token: 	: Key with algorithm RS512 and type RSA is incorrect for provider algorithm RS256
at org.keycloak.crypto.SignatureProvider.checkKeyForVerification(SignatureProvider.java:33)

{
        kid: '3',
        kty: 'RSA',
        alg: 'PS512',
        use: 'sig',
        e: 'AQAB',
        n: '2s3t1LBJxVpq......',
        d: 'Hv4aWQ0bdf.....',
        p: '9rjHyDLJllALP......',
        q: '4whieZi3S7y......',
        dp: 'i160VG4EHCoZP2.....',
        dq: 'TZ5iPHxPvl79fD.....',
        qi: '5maXjtEVKJGlrQ......',
      },
      {
        kid: '3',
        kty: 'RSA',
        alg: 'RS256',
        use: 'sig',
        e: 'AQAB',
        n: '2s3t1LBJxVpq......',
        d: 'Hv4aWQ0bdf.....',
        p: '9rjHyDLJllALP......',
        q: '4whieZi3S7y......',
        dp: 'i160VG4EHCoZP2.....',
        dq: 'TZ5iPHxPvl79fD.....',
        qi: '5maXjtEVKJGlrQ......',
      },
      {
        kid: '3',
        kty: 'RSA',
        alg: 'RS512',
        use: 'sig',
        e: 'AQAB',
        n: '2s3t1LBJxVpq......',
        d: 'Hv4aWQ0bdf.....',
        p: '9rjHyDLJllALP......',
        q: '4whieZi3S7y......',
        dp: 'i160VG4EHCoZP2.....',
        dq: 'TZ5iPHxPvl79fD.....',
        qi: '5maXjtEVKJGlrQ......',
      }

### Anything else?

The bug appears to be in the way the method:
```
org.keycloak.util.JWKSUtils.getKeyWrappersForUse(JSONWebKeySet keySet, JWK.Use requestedUse)
``` 
creates the map of keys from the jwks - the 'kid' alone is the key to the map so the last key in the jwks with the same 'kid' overwrites the others.

NOTE: It _is_ valid to have a list of keys in a jwks that have the same 'use', 'kid' and 'kty' but different 'alg' (algorithm) see the standard at:
https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7517#:~:text=member%20is%20OPTIONAL.-,4.5.%20%22kid%22%20(key%20id)%20parameter,-The%20%22kid%22%20(key
commit_message:
Support for multiple keys with same kid, which differ just by algorithm in the JWKS (#15114)

Closes #14794

issue_title: User password is visible on admin events tab
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

This issue exists in keycloak for a very long time.
I may create a user with pre-defined password via REST API.
I will see a request body on admin events tab with clear text password in case `Include Representation` option is activated for admin events.

### Version

19.0.2

### Expected behavior

The `representation` field of `admin_event_entity` table shouldn't contain a clear text password

### Actual behavior

The `representation` field of `admin_event_entity` table contains a clear text password

### How to Reproduce?

1. Turn on `Include Representation` option for admin events
2. Execute this query
```
curl -i -X POST \
   -H "Content-Type:application/json" \
   -d \
'{
  "username": "test.test@gmail.com",
  "enabled": true,
  "email": "test.test@gmail.com",
  "credentials": [
    {
      "type": "password",
      "value": "my strong password"
    }
  ]
}' \
 'http://localhost:8080/auth/admin/realms/test-realm/users'
```
3. check `representation` field of `admin_event_entity` table
4. you will see value `my strong password`

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Client session lifespan doesn't consider user session lifespan
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

We're using rememberMe option set for 180 days in our realm and we noticed a lot of garbage client sessions due to that.
We investigated that method getClientSessionLifespanMs of SessionTimeouts class doesn't consider expiration of user session. So for example, if user session was initiated 170 days ago and client session is being refreshed it will be places into Infinispan with lifespan of another 180 days as client session has always refreshed timestamp that doesn't consider first user session creation. After 10 days, user session is correctly evicted but for next 170 days the client session is there unused.
With ~1.6 millions 'rememberMe' user sessions we end up with ~3 mln garbage client sessions every 2 weeks.

### Version

15.1.1

### Expected behavior

Client session lifespan is not longer than expiration of related user session lifespan.

### Actual behavior

Client session lifespan is always set with lifespan considering current timestamp + rememberMe period.

### How to Reproduce?

Turn on rememberMe option on realm.
Login and create both user & client sessions. 
Then after expiration time, refresh session - user session correctly uses initiated timestamp to compute lifespan but client session uses current timestamp.

### Anything else?

I checked that there are no changes in later Keycloak releases.

issue_title: Weird export/re-import behaviour regarding `post.logout.redirect.uris`
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

We started seeing invalid redirect uri errors during logout in some of our environments.

I found out that the erroneous ones were missing the `+` setting in `post.logout.redirect.uris` / `Valid post logout redirect URIs`.
These environments don't use our realm import optimization (which we only use for dialy dev support to swiftly spin up a fresh KC container).
The dev environments which do use that import were not affected.

That's when I found a weird behaviour:
- bootstrap a fresh KC container and populate our realm with our (slow) script that executes `kcadm.sh` many times
- check admin console (v2): custom client does *not* have the `+` setting!
- export the realm via `kc.sh export --file  ... --users same_file`
- check the exported json: `post.logout.redirect.uris` is missing entirely for the custom client (consistent with what you see in UI)
- throw away the container and its database
- bootstrap a fresh KC container but this time let it import the previously exported file (via `--import-realm`)
- check admin console (v2): custom client *now does* have the `+` setting!
- export the realm and check the json: the custom client now has `"post.logout.redirect.uris": "+"` (consistent with what you see in UI)

Why is the `+` added on realm import?
Or coming from the opposite side: Why isn't it there from the beginning?

This effect is the reason why we found the problem so late on our non-dev environments; every dev was using the import and logout was fine there...

### Version

19.0.3

### Expected behavior

Consistent handling of the `+` setting, symmetric Import/Export.
To have a working logout OOTB I'd say `+` should be the default/initial value but I'm probably missing something.

### Actual behavior

Asymmetric import/export; `+` is added during import for unknown reasons (maybe because realm imports from older KC versions shall receive `+` automatically?)

### How to Reproduce?

See description.

### Anything else?

cc @mposolda @tgerakitis
commit_message:
Weird export/re-import behaviour regarding post.logout.redirect.uris
Closes #14884

issue_title: jwks endpoint for X/Y coordinates in EC keypair can return less bytes than expected
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

The [RFC-7518](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7518#section-6.2.1.2) specifies that X and Y fields for a EC key MUST be the full size of the curve coordinate specified in the "crv" parameter.

>   The "x" (x coordinate) parameter contains the x coordinate for the
>   Elliptic Curve point.  It is represented as the base64url encoding of
>   the octet string representation of the coordinate, as defined in
>   [Section 2.3.5](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7518#section-2.3.5) of [SEC1](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7518#ref-SEC1) [SEC1].  The length of this octet string MUST
>   be the full size of a coordinate for the curve specified in the "crv"
>   parameter.  For example, if the value of "crv" is "P-521", the octet
>   string must be 66 octets long.

In current implementation the big integer is always written removing the sign bit using [toIntegerBytes](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/19.0.3/core/src/main/java/org/keycloak/jose/jwk/JWKUtil.java#L29). So it can happen that the resulting bytes are less than expected. Example test:

```java
    @Test
    void test() throws NoSuchAlgorithmException, InvalidKeySpecException {
        byte[] bytes = Base64.getDecoder().decode("MIGbMBAGByqGSM49AgEGBSuBBAAjA4GGAAQA17IdNXGUWR9trV1RjjtU4yQiHG1LvpVGyuqQ8t8t2ei3ao/dEu+h2RVPUlD2JuqQfbEIb0jeS5pXgJlO/HSLBYoATBvn23AmBEF7LmLR8uNTW+nJcwm1Tg2sVKvQ7i202Y5TkhvsmwwKx9U/+K6HgB22m7FqhtLxFR8InSmMnnK/0sU="); //EC publickey whose y coordinate byte array start with 0
        X509EncodedKeySpec keyspec = new X509EncodedKeySpec(bytes);
        KeyFactory keyFactory = KeyFactory.getInstance("EC");
        ECPublicKey publicKey = (ECPublicKey) keyFactory.generatePublic(keyspec);
        BigInteger bigInteger = publicKey.getW().getAffineY();
        byte[] yBytes = toIntegerBytes(bigInteger);
        assertThat(yBytes).hasSize(66); // should be 66, but 65
    }
```

### Version

19.0.3

### Expected behavior

The X and Y coordinates in the JWKS should be always 66 bytes when using P-521.

### Actual behavior

Sometime it can be 65 (if the first byte starts by 0).

### How to Reproduce?

_No response_

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
jwks endpoint for X/Y coordinates in EC keypair can return less bytes than expected (#14952)

Closes #14933
